lines.gurmukhi: ਦਿਨ ਮਹਿ ਰੈਣਿ ਰੈਣਿ ਮਹਿ ਦਿਨੀਅਰੁ ਉਸਨ ਸੀਤ ਬਿਧਿ ਸੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: din meh rain rain meh dineear usan seet bidh soee |
lines.english: The night is in the day, and the day is in the night. The same is true of hot and cold.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸ ਤੇ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਪਰਮ ਗਤਿ ਹੋਈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: tis te mukat param gat hoee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Through this, the supreme state of liberation is attained. ||1||Pause||
How rare are those who contemplate this spiritual wisdom.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਕੀ ਗਤਿ ਮਿਤਿ ਅਵਰੁ ਨ ਜਾਣੈ ਗੁਰ ਬਿਨੁ ਸਮਝ ਨ ਹੋਈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: taa kee gat mit avar na jaanai gur bin samajh na hoee |2|
lines.english: No one else knows His state and extent; without the Guru, this is not understood. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੁਰਖ ਮਹਿ ਨਾਰਿ ਨਾਰਿ ਮਹਿ ਪੁਰਖਾ ਬੂਝਹੁ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗਿਆਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: purakh meh naar naar meh purakhaa boojhahu braham giaanee |
lines.english: The female is in the male, and the male is in the female. Understand this, O God-realized being!

lines.gurmukhi: ਧੁਨਿ ਮਹਿ ਧਿਆਨੁ ਧਿਆਨ ਮਹਿ ਜਾਨਿਆ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਅਕਥ ਕਹਾਨੀ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: dhun meh dhiaan dhiaan meh jaaniaa guramukh akath kahaanee |3|
lines.english: The meditation is in the music, and knowledge is in meditation. Become Gurmukh, and speak the Unspoken Speech. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਮਹਿ ਜੋਤਿ ਜੋਤਿ ਮਹਿ ਮਨੂਆ ਪੰਚ ਮਿਲੇ ਗੁਰ ਭਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: man meh jot jot meh manooaa panch mile gur bhaaee |
lines.english: The Light is in the mind, and the mind is in the Light. The Guru brings the five senses together, like brothers.
Raamkalee, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਤਿਨ ਕੈ ਸਦ ਬਲਿਹਾਰੀ ਜਿਨ ਏਕ ਸਬਦਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਈ ॥੪॥੯॥
lines.translit: naanak tin kai sad balihaaree jin ek sabad liv laaee |4|9|
lines.english: Nanak is forever a sacrifice to those who enshrine love for the One Word of the Shabad. ||4||9||
When the Lord God showered His Mercy,
egotism was eradicated from within me.
That humble servant who contemplates the
Word of the Guru's Shabad, is very dear to the Lord. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਹਰਿ ਜਨੁ ਹਰਿ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਭਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: so har jan har prabh bhaavai |
lines.english: That humble servant of the Lord is pleasing to his Lord God;

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਭਗਤਿ ਕਰੇ ਦਿਨੁ ਰਾਤੀ ਲਾਜ ਛੋਡਿ ਹਰਿ ਕੇ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਵੈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: ahinis bhagat kare din raatee laaj chhodd har ke gun gaavai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: day and night, he performs devotional worship, day and night. Disregarding his own honor, he sings the Glorious Praises of the Lord. ||1||Pause||
The unstruck melody of the sound current resonates and resounds;
my mind is appeased by the subtle essence of the Lord.
Through the Perfect Guru, I am absorbed in Truth.
Through the Guru, I have found the Lord, the Primal Being. ||2||
My mind is attuned to the Lord of the Universe.
Gurbani is the sound current of the Naad, the Vedas, everything.
One whose self-conceit is gone, sees his fears run away.
He is my sacred shrine of pilgrimage, fasting and austere self-discipline.
The Lord saves, and carries across, those who meet with the Guru. ||3||
That servant grasps the Guru's feet.
Says Nanak, I have merged into the Word of the Shabad. ||4||10||
The Guru, the True Guru, has expelled my doubts.
He burns with hunger and corruption, and will suffer in the world hereafter.
Raamkalee, First Mehl:
He runs around, begging for clothes and food.
Only through the Guru's Teachings will such a person become devoted. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਨਹੀ ਲੀਨੀ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਪਤਿ ਖੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: guramat nahee leenee duramat pat khoee |
lines.english: He does not follow the Guru's Teachings; through his evil-mindedness, he loses his honor.
The way of the Yogi is to dwell in the celestial home of bliss.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਏਕੋ ਕਰਿ ਦੇਖਿਆ ਭੀਖਿਆ ਭਾਇ ਸਬਦਿ ਤ੍ਰਿਪਤਾਸੈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: ek drisatt eko kar dekhiaa bheekhiaa bhaae sabad tripataasai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: He looks impartially, equally upon all. He receives the charity of the Lord's Love, and the Word of the Shabad, and so he is satisfied. ||1||Pause||
But when the axle breaks, the wagon falls and crashes.
By the Lord's power, one's honor is preserved.
The five bulls, the senses, pull the wagon of the body around.
It falls apart, like a pile of logs. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਖੁ ਸੁਖੁ ਸਮ ਕਰਣਾ ਸੋਗ ਬਿਓਗੀ ॥
lines.translit: dukh sukh sam karanaa sog biogee |
lines.english: Look upon pain and pleasure as one and the same, sorrow and separation.
Contemplate the Word of the Guru's Shabad, Yogi.
Let your food be contemplative meditation upon the Naam, the Name of the Lord, and the Word of the Guru's Shabad.
Wear the loin-cloth of poise, and be free of entanglements.
Your wall shall be permanent, by meditating on the Formless Lord. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਮਹਿ ਮੁੰਦ੍ਰਾ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਰ ਸਰਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: man meh mundraa har gur saranaa |
lines.english: In your mind, let your ear-rings be the Sanctuary of the Guru, the Lord.
The Guru's Word shall release you from sexual desire and anger.
O Nanak, worshipping the Lord in deep devotion, the humble are carried across. ||4||11||
